name: Viewpoints of Fraile and Picón de Felipe – Natural Park of Arribes del Duero
name_original: Miradores del Fraile y del Picón de Felipe – Parque Natural de las Arribes del Duero
region_intro: Hiking around Aldeadávila De La Ribera offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, characterized by the deep Duero River canyon. This region features steep riverbeds contrasting with the surrounding hilly relief, composed of granitoids, schists, and slates. Notable natural features include the impressive Aldeadávila Dam and the Pozo de los Humos Waterfall on the Uces River. The terrain provides diverse hiking experiences, from riverside paths to elevated viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Aldeadávila De La Ribera The most popular hiking route is Viewpoints of Fraile and Picón de Felipe – Natural Park of Arribes del Duero, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views of the Duero canyon and the Aldeadávila Dam. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rupurapay Viewpoint Trail, a moderate 4.3 miles (7.0 km) path. This trail leads to the Rupurapay viewpoint, providing impressive vistas of the Duero River canyon. Local hikers also love the Friar's Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila Dam, a 1.3 miles (2.1 km) trail leading through the canyon landscape, often completed in about 39 minutes. Hiking around Aldeadávila De La Ribera is defined by the majestic Duero River canyon, numerous panoramic viewpoints, and the Aldeadávila Dam. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging treks. The routes in Aldeadávila De La Ribera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 60 reviews. More than 470 hikers have used komoot to explore Aldeadávila De La Ribera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aldeadávila De La Ribera?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in Aldeadávila De La Ribera, offering a variety of experiences within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Aldeadávila De La Ribera?
A: The terrain is characterized by the dramatic Duero River canyon, featuring deep, steep riverbeds that contrast with the gentler, hilly relief of the surrounding Northern Plateau. You'll encounter geological formations of granitoids, schists, and slates, with paths ranging from riverside routes to elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Aldeadávila De La Ribera?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy hiking routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17503601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Friar's Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.1 km trail that takes about 39 minutes to complete.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore significant natural features such as the majestic Duero River canyon and the impressive Aldeadávila Dam. A must-see is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Uces River, known for its misty 'smoke' effect.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints along the trails?
A: The region is famous for its panoramic viewpoints. You can find stunning vistas at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the Duero canyon and Aldeadávila Dam, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4820142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupurapay Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides impressive perspectives of the Duero River canyon and Cascada de Remolino.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17503601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Friar's Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila Dam&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.1 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10411210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Pinero – Pozo de los Humos Waterfall loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.1 km option.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Aldeadávila De La Ribera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic canyon landscapes, the breathtaking viewpoints, and the diverse trails that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Is it possible to visit the Pozo de los Humos Waterfall?
A: Yes, the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4230399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de los Humos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a major attraction. You can reach it via trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10411210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Pinero – Pozo de los Humos Waterfall loop from Aldeadávila de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive experience of the area.

Q: Are the trails in Aldeadávila De La Ribera suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails are suitable for families, especially the easier, shorter loops that lead to viewpoints without significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17503601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Friar's Viewpoint loop from Aldeadávila Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for a family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aldeadávila De La Ribera?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. During these seasons, the weather is ideal for exploring the canyon and enjoying the viewpoints. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: The Arribes del Duero Natural Park generally welcomes dogs on trails, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near cliffs and livestock. Always check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, there are 4 difficult routes for experienced trekkers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993150466/viewpoints-of-fraile-and-picon-de-felipe-natural-park-of-arribes-del-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoints of Fraile and Picón de Felipe – Natural Park of Arribes del Duero&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.8 km trail with significant elevation changes, offering expansive views of the Duero canyon.
tips: Panoramic point with one of the best views of the Duero canyon. Located within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, it can be reached by a path with technical sections from the car park right at the foot of the Saucelle dam or by extending the route from Aldeadávila de la Ribera.

The views from here are amazing! One of the most impressive stretches of the Duero canyon as it passes through the Salamanca region. This place is worth visiting and you can get here by car or by following one of the paths from Aldeadávila de la Ribera or Mieza.
